questions.text: The Triple Point of water is at:
questions.explanation: The temperature at which ice, water, and water vapor coexist in equilibrium.

questions.text: Coefficient of linear expansion (α), area expansion (β), and volume expansion (γ) are related as:
questions.explanation: β ≈ 2α and γ ≈ 3α for isotropic solids.

questions.text: Which substance has the highest specific heat capacity?
questions.explanation: Water has an exceptionally high specific heat (~4186 J/kg·K).

questions.text: The heat required to change the state of a substance without temperature change is:
questions.explanation: Q = mL. Latent means 'hidden'.

questions.text: Newton's Law of Cooling is applicable when the temperature difference is:
questions.explanation: Rate of loss of heat is proportional to temperature difference for small differences.

questions.text: In which mode of heat transfer is no medium required?
questions.explanation: Radiation travels through vacuum as electromagnetic waves.

questions.text: Wien's Displacement Law relates the peak wavelength (λ) to absolute temperature (T) as:
questions.explanation: As T increases, peak wavelength shifts towards shorter wavelengths (blue).

questions.text: The amount of heat radiated by a black body per unit area per unit time is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Stefan-Boltzmann Law: E = σT⁴.

questions.text: Thermal conductivity (k) depends on:
questions.explanation: It is a characteristic property of the material.

questions.text: An anomalous expansion is shown by water between:
questions.explanation: Water contracts on heating and expands on cooling in this range; density is max at 4°C.

questions.text: Heat transfer in liquids and gases primarily occurs by:
questions.explanation: Involves the actual movement of heated particles.

questions.text: The SI unit of Thermal Conductivity is:
questions.explanation: From Q/t = kAΔT/L.

questions.text: Which of the following temperature scales does not have negative values?
questions.explanation: Kelvin scale starts from absolute zero (0 K).

questions.text: At what temperature do the Celsius and Fahrenheit scales coincide?
questions.explanation: Using C/5 = (F-32)/9, setting C=F gives x = -40.

questions.text: Specific heat of a gas in an isothermal process is:
questions.explanation: s = Q / mΔT. Since ΔT = 0, s is infinite.

questions.text: A perfect black body is one which:
options: Absorbs all radiation falling on it
questions.explanation: Its absorption coefficient is 1.

questions.text: The rate of loss of heat from a body depends on:
questions.explanation: Based on cooling laws and Stefan's law.

questions.text: A bimetallic strip works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Two different metals expand differently, causing the strip to bend.

questions.text: Regelation is the phenomenon of:
questions.explanation: Refreezing occurs when pressure is released.

questions.text: Cooking is faster in a pressure cooker because:
questions.explanation: Increased pressure raises the boiling point, allowing water to reach higher temperatures.

questions.text: Temperature is a measure of:
questions.explanation: Higher temperature means faster molecular motion.

questions.text: Heat flows from high temperature to low temperature. This is related to:
questions.explanation: Entropy and direction of heat flow.

questions.text: Units of Latent Heat are:
questions.explanation: Q = mL => L = Q/m.

questions.text: Emissivity of a perfect black body is:
questions.explanation: It is a perfect emitter as well as a perfect absorber.

questions.text: Greenhouse effect is due to absorption of:
questions.explanation: Earth's surface radiates IR, which is trapped by CO₂ and other gases.

questions.text: Absolute Zero is the temperature at which:
questions.explanation: Theoretical temperature where kinetic energy is zero.

questions.text: Relation between C and K is:
questions.explanation: Kelvin is Celsius shifted by 273.15.

questions.text: Molar heat capacity at constant pressure (Cp) is _____ than Cv.
questions.explanation: Cp = Cv + R. Extra heat is needed for expansion work.

questions.text: Calorimeters are usually made of:
questions.explanation: Copper has low specific heat and is a good conductor, reaching equilibrium quickly.

questions.text: Which color absorbs the most heat?
questions.explanation: Black is the best absorber of all wavelengths.

questions.text: Good absorbers are good emitters. This is:
questions.explanation: Basic law of radiation.

questions.text: Thermos flask prevents heat loss by:
questions.explanation: Vacuum, silvering, and cork are used.

questions.text: Specific heat of water is:
questions.explanation: Standard values.

questions.text: Unit of Stefan constant (σ) is:
questions.explanation: From E = σT⁴.

questions.text: Latent heat of fusion of ice is approx:
questions.explanation: Value for melting ice.

questions.text: Latent heat of vaporization of water is approx:
questions.explanation: Requires much more energy than melting.

questions.text: Thermal expansion of gases is _____ than solids.
questions.explanation: Weak intermolecular forces.

questions.text: Cooling is caused by:
questions.explanation: Evaporating molecules take heat from the surface.

questions.text: Heat capacity is:
questions.explanation: Depends on mass of object.

questions.text: Dimensional formula of Specific Heat is:
questions.explanation: Heat/Mass*Temp.
